tool_name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure

inputs.name: Design (Maximum) Volume Flow Rate at Operating Conditions (Q_op)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Fluid Density at Operating Conditions (ρ)
inputs.note: Gas: use operating density ρ = PM/(ZRT); liquid: use process temperature density
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Fluid Dynamic Viscosity at Operating Conditions (μ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Maximum Allowable Differential Pressure (ΔP_max)
inputs.note: Transmitter range; higher ΔP → better accuracy; lower ΔP → lower permanent pressure loss
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Pressure Tap Type

options.label: Corner taps — ISO 5167 standard, most common
Flange taps (1 inch from faces) — ASME / AGA 3

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Gas Isentropic Exponent (κ) — Set 1.0 for liquid
inputs.note: Liquid κ = 1.0 (no expansibility correction); air κ = 1.4; steam ≈ 1.3; natural gas ≈ 1.27
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_pipe_m_s = design_flow_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re_D = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)   // [m/s, dimensionless] Pipe Velocity & Reynolds Number at Design Flow Reference: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6.1 — pipe Reynolds number determines discharge coefficient validity

Cd_est = 0.6; epsilon = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - 0.41 * Math.pow(0.5, 1/isentropic_exponent) : 1.0; beta = Math.pow(1 / (1 + Math.pow(Cd_est * epsilon * A_pipe_m2 * Math.sqrt(2 * max_dP_kpa * 1000 / fluid_density_kg_m3) / (design_flow_m3h / 3600), 2)), 0.25)   // [dimensionless] Required Beta Ratio (β) from ΔP and Flow Reference: ISO 5167-2 §5 — iterate β from Q = Cd·ε·(π/4)·d²·√(2ΔP/ρ·(1−β⁴)^−0.5); initial Cd=0.60

b4 = Math.pow(beta, 4); A_coef = (19000 * beta / Re_D) < 1 ? 0 : 0.0188 + 0.0063 * Math.pow(19000 * beta / Re_D, 0.8); Cd = 0.5961 + 0.0261 * beta * beta - 0.216 * Math.pow(beta, 8) + 0.000521 * Math.pow(1e6 * beta / Re_D, 0.7) + (0.0188 + 0.0063 * A_coef) * Math.pow(beta, 3.5) * Math.pow(1e6 / Re_D, 0.3)   // [dimensionless] Refined Discharge Coefficient — Reader-Harris/Gallagher (RHG) Reference: ISO 5167-2:2022 Eq.(1) — Reader-Harris/Gallagher correlation for corner taps; uncertainty ±0.5% for 0.1 ≤ β ≤ 0.75
d_mm = beta * pipe_ID_mm   // [mm] Orifice Bore Diameter (d) Reference: d = β·D — orifice throat diameter

epsilon_act = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - (0.351 + 0.256 * Math.pow(beta, 4) + 0.93 * Math.pow(beta, 8)) * (1 - Math.pow(1 - max_dP_kpa * 1000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * v_pipe_m_s / 2 * 100), 1/isentropic_exponent)) : 1.0; dP_actual_kpa = Math.pow(design_flow_m3h / 3600 / (Cd * epsilon_act * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(beta, 2) / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4))), 2) * fluid_density_kg_m3 / 2 / 1000   // [kPa] Actual Differential Pressure at Design Flow (ΔP_actual) Reference: ISO 5167-2 Eq.(4) — rearranged for ΔP given Q, Cd, β, ε

E_factor = 1 / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4)); dP_perm_kpa = dP_actual_kpa * (1 - Math.pow(beta, 2)) * (1 - Cd * Math.pow(beta, 2) * E_factor)   // [kPa, dimensionless] Permanent Pressure Loss (ΔP_perm) & Velocity of Approach Factor Reference: ISO 5167-2 §5.1.7 — permanent pressure loss: ΔP_perm = ΔP·(1−β²)·(1−Cd·β²·E)

V1.error_msg: Beta ratio outside 0.20–0.75: ISO 5167-2 validity range exceeded — accuracy degrades sharply; select ΔP range to keep β in 0.30–0.65 for optimal accuracy

V2.error_msg: Pipe Reynolds number < 5000: ISO 5167 RHG equation loses accuracy below Re = 5000; consider Coriolis or magnetic flowmeter for low-Re applications

V3.error_msg: Permanent pressure loss > 80% of ΔP_max: high energy penalty — consider low-loss flow element (Venturi, V-cone, or Annubar) to reduce permanent loss

smart_warnings.message: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%

fmea.effect: Beta ratio outside 0.20–0.75: ISO 5167-2 validity range exceeded — accuracy degrades sharply; select ΔP range to keep β in 0.30–0.65 for optimal accuracy
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Pipe Reynolds number < 5000: ISO 5167 RHG equation loses accuracy below Re = 5000; consider Coriolis or magnetic flowmeter for low-Re applications
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Permanent pressure loss > 80% of ΔP_max: high energy penalty — consider low-loss flow element (Venturi, V-cone, or Annubar) to reduce permanent loss
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_pipe_m_s > 30 && fluid_density_kg_m3 < 5

fmea.failureMode: Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dP_actual_kpa > max_dP_kpa * 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Re_D < 20000 && beta > 0.5
